prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='CWT_svr', recommended_method='inverse_rmse_weights'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal a strong upward trend across all periods with significant seasonal amplitude (~99). CWT_svr dominates seasonality capture (0.5081 corr), while DWT_catbest best tracks trends. Early horizons favor NaiveSeasonal/CWT_catboost, but late horizons prefer THETA/FT_rf—this specialization difference suggests per-horizon selection could help. However, with rmse_spread_ratio at 0.27 (<0.4) and no high model disagreement, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality-based weighting is recommended to balance the strong seasonal variance (0.6361) across models.

raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.128 suggests moderate disagreement (not high), so inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage is appropriate. Pattern analyst recommends inverse_rmse_weights due to seasonality_corr_variance=0.6361. ARIMA has best RMSE (92.30) vs ONLY_CWT_rf worst (118.65), giving rmse_spread_ratio=0.27 (<0.4). Selection + weighted + stacking diversity captures both trend champions (DWT_catboost) and seasonality champions (CWT_svr) across horizons."
}
